A VERY BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG GA HWY 215 SOUTH AND FALCON CIRCLE, ABOUT 8 MILES SOUTH OF ABBEVILLE DURING THE LATE AFTERNOON HOURS OF SUNDAY, JANUARY 2ND. THE TORNADO, WHICH SHOWED A VERY WEAK DEBRIS SIGNATURE FROM KJGX SNAPPED AND UPROOTED SEVERAL LARGE PINES ON WITHIN THE FALCON CIRCLE AREA, PARTICULARLY NEAR THE SOUTH ENTRANCE/EXIT OFF 215. IN ADDITION TO TREE DAMAGE, A MEDIUM-SIZED SHED WAS DESTROYED ALONG WITH SOME TIN FROM THE ROOF OF ONE OF THE HOMES BEING PULLED OFF. MAX WINDS

A VERY BRIEF QLCS TORNADO HIT THE SOUTH SIDE OF VIDALIA AROUND 7PM ON SUNDAY, JANUARY 2ND. THE TORNADO DID THE MOST DAMAGE TO ONE OF THE VIDALIA FIRE STATION'S OFF OF AIMWELL ROAD. THE GARAGE DOORS WERE BLOWN INWARD AND OUTWARD ALONG WITH A PORTION OF THE ROOF AND SIDING PULLED OFF. DEBRIS WAS THROWN ABOUT 200 YARDS DOWNSTREAM FROM THIS POINT WHERE ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SEEN SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THIS DAMAGE PUTS PEAK WINDS AROUND 75 MPH, AN EF-0 RATING

AN EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED ACROSS FAR EASTERN TWIGGS INTO SOUTHWEST WILKINSON COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE, SOME SIGNIFICANT, ALONG THE TRACK 12.4 MILE LONG TRACK.

AN EF-1 TORNADO QUICKLY DEVELOPED ON THE SOUTHEAST SIDE OF COCHRAN. A NUMBER OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN COCHRAN. SEVERAL HOMES ALSO EXPERIENCED DAMAGE ALONG THE 7.7 MILE TRACK WITH THE 2ND STORY OF ONE HOME REMOVED.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED WATER IN THE ROADWAY AT HIGHWAY 101 SOUTH AT CRIDER CREEK DRIVE NEAR CRIDER CREEK. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 1.5 AND 2 INCHES OF RAIN OCCURRED QUICKLY OVER THE AREA.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLOODING OF MULTIPLE SECTIONS OF PAUL HARRIS ROAD BETWEEN MARSHALL FULLER ROAD AND CUMBERLAND PATH. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 1.5 AND 2 INCHES OF RAIN OCCURRED, RESULTING IN FLOODING FROM SHED CREEK/SWAMPY AREAS.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED THAT FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF PIEDMONT ROAD NE AND MIAMI CIRCLE NE CAUSED A CAR TO STALL. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 2 AND 3 INCHES OF RAIN ACCUMULATED QUICKLY IN THE AREA.
ROADS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING NEAR WOODSTOCK ROAD AND JAKE TAYLOR DRIVE.
THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLOODING AT MCKEOWN ROAD AT MILLERTOWN ROAD. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT 1.5 TO 2 INCHES OF RAINFALL OCCURRED OVER THE AREA, RESULTING IN THE FLASH FLOODING.
ROAD CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING AT DAVID LEE STREET AND VICTORY DRIVE.
NWS SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE COMMUNITY OF MAUK. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE TRACK AND SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE ALONG THE 3.1 MILE TRACK.
NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG HWY 90 NEAR HENRY CURRINGTON RD IN NORTHWEST TAYLOR COUNTY
THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLOODING IN THE INTERSECTION OF CUMMING HIGHWAY AT KEY DRIVE.
THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLOODING OF WATER TANK ROAD FROM CANTON CREEK.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING NEAR GRADY HOSPITAL THAT TEMPORARILY CUT OFF ACCESS TO EMERGENCY VEHICLES. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 2 AND 3 INCHES OCCURRED QUICKLY OVER THE AREA.

ANOTHER EF-1 TORNADO FORMED FROM THE SAME STORM THAT PRODUCED THE WESTERN TAYLOR COUNTY TORNADO. THE TORNADO REACHED PEAK INTENSITY NEAR FICKLING MILL WHERE LARGE GROVES OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND THE MILL SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN.
THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLOODING NEAR ALLENE AVE AND AVON THAT STALLED A VEHICLE, STRANDING ITS OCCUPANT. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 2 AND 3 INCHES OF RAIN HAD OCCURRED AT THE TIME.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, DOWNED TREES, AND DOWNED POWERLINES ALONG SR 137 BETWEEN SR 208 NORTH TO CROWELL ROAD.
STRUCTURES DAMAGED AT THE TYSON FEED MILL. MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWNED.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE FOUNTAINVILLE COMMUNITY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 17.5 MILE TRACK WITH ADDITIONAL MINOR DAMAGE TO A TYSON PLANT AND A MOBILE HOME AS WELL AS OUTBUILDINGS AT WHITEWATER STATE PARK.
SEVERAL BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES AT THE WATER PARK. MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES WERE DOWN BLOCKING THE ROAD.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWNED ALONG MONCRIEF RD AND HWY 128.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AS IT APPROACHED ROBERTA ALONG HIGHWAY 128. A NUMBER OF HOMES SUSTAINED ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE ALONG THE TRACK WITH NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DESTROYED.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AT HIGHWAY 90 AND LOBLOLLY RD.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 341.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AT WHITEWATER RD. AND HWY 128.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED THAT TWO OCCUPANTS HAD TO BE RESCUED FROM A VEHICLE THAT BECAME STRANDED IN FLOODWATERS ON E PINE VALLEY ROAD NW. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 2.5 AND 3 INCHES OF RAIN HAD ACCUMULATED OVER THE AREA.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED THE INTERSECTION OF LYNHURST DR SW AND MARTIN LUTHER KING JR DR SW IMPASSABLE AND CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT BETWEEN 2.5 AND 3 INCHES OCCURRED OVER THE AREA.

AN EF-2 TORNADO DEVELOPED EAST OF BYROMVILLE WHERE FIVE LARGE, WELL-ANCHORED GRAIN BINS WERE DESTROYED. BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DESTROYED ALONG WITH NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. A UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA MESONET STATION MEASURED A 129.3 MPH WIND GUST AS THE TORNADO HIT.
WEATHER INSTRUMENTATION HEAVILY DAMAGED AND GRAIN SILOS WERE DESTROYED.

DOOLY COUNTY EM REPORTED LIKELY TORNADO DAMAGE STARTING NEAR KENT SMITH RD EAST OF BYROMVILLE AND NEAR CONTINUOUS PATH OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN EAST FROM THAT POINT. FIVE GRAIN BINS DESTROYED, NUMEROUS TREES AND LINES DOWNED, SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND BARNS DAMAGED, SOME HOMES WITH MINOR DAMAGE.

4 BARNS WERE DESTROYED, 4 BARNS WITH MAJOR DAMAGE, 8 HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE, 3 SMALL GRAIN SILOS WERE DESTROYED, 4 BARN OUT BUILDINGS SUSTAINED DAMAGE AND 5 PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WERE FLIPPED. DAMAGE MOSTLY OCCURRED FROM THE INTERSECTION OF KENT SMITH ROAD AND NELLIEVILLE ROAD NORTHEASTWARD TO COLLINS ROAD.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN WITH TRAFFIC SIGNALS OUT NEAR INTERSECTION OF RIVOLLI DR AND FORSYTH
HOME DESTROYED, TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ON THORNWOOD DRIVE NEAR ROSA TAYLOR ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. TDS CONFIRMED ON RADAR IN THIS AREA.

*** 1 INJ *** A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AS IT APPROACHED BONAIRE AND RAPIDLY INTENSIFIED INTO EF-3 STRENGTH. TWO LARGE HIGH-TENSION POWER LINE TOWERS WERE DESTROYED ALONG WITH A LARGE CONCRETE POWER POLE. MULTIPLE HOMES EXPERIENCED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE, AND ONE WAS DESTROYED.
REPORT OF 1 HOME DESTROYED WITH POWERLINES DOWN.
RESIDENT REPORTS APPROXIMATELY 100 TREES DOWN ON PROPERTY AT THE 400 BLOCK OF OLD MONTICELLO RD.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN WEST-NORTHWEST OF MILLEDGEVILLE. MORE THAN 100 TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN AROUND A PROPERTY JUST SOUTH OF THE OLD MONTICELLO ROAD NW AND AVERY STORE ROAD INTERSECTION, WITH ADDITIONAL TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ABOUT 1/2 MILE UP ALONG AVERY STORE ROAD. A WEAK TDS WAS EVIDENT FROM RADAR AS THE CIRCULATION MOVED NNE.
SEVERAL TREES REPORTED DOWN ON AVERY RD NEAR OLD MONTICELLO RD.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 441.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED ACROSS FAR EASTERN TWIGGS INTO SOUTHWEST WILKINSON COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE, SOME SIGNIFICANT, ALONG THE TRACK 12.4 MILE LONG TRACK.

AN EF-1 TORNADO QUICKLY DEVELOPED ON THE SOUTHEAST SIDE OF COCHRAN. A NUMBER OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN COCHRAN. SEVERAL HOMES ALSO EXPERIENCED DAMAGE ALONG THE 7.7 MILE TRACK WITH THE 2ND STORY OF ONE HOME REMOVED.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A ROOF OF A ONE-STORY HOME JUST OFF OLD EASTMAN ROAD NEAR THE BLECKLEY AND DODGE COUNTY LINE. THE TORNADO HAD ALREADY BEEN DOWN FOR 1-2 MINUTES BEFORE THIS AREA WAS HIT.
MINOR FLOODING AT A RESIDENCE ON THE 600 BLOCK OF STEMBRIDGE RD.
TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF W CHICKEN ROAD AND RODDY HWY IN DODGE COUNTY.
TREES DOWN AND A SMALL SHED DESTROYED ALONG SIBBIE ROAD, NORTH OF AMERICAN LEGION ROAD.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 129 SOUTH.

THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLOODING FROM THE FLINT RIVER THAT IMPACTED ARROWHEAD BLVD, UPPER RIVERDALE ROAD, GARDEN WALK BLVD AND RESIDENCES ALONG ROXBURY DR. FLOODING RANGED FROM 0.5-1 FOOT ALONG THE STREETS, AND 3 FEET IN THE BACK YARDS ALONG ROXBURY DR.
SEVERAL TREES KNOCKED DOWN AROUND WARRENTON. ONE TREE CAME THROUGH MOBILE HOME OFF PARKWAY DRIVE PER EM REPORT. NO KNOWN INJURIES.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWNED.
METAL ROOFING PULLED OFF A BARN/SHED ALONG WIRE ROAD. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG OHOOPEE BEND.

A FAST MOVING TORNADO BEGAN IN FIVE POINTS IN TREUTLEN CO, TRAVELED NNE DESTROYING SEVERAL BARNS, OUTBUILDINGS, WITH HEAVY STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO MULTIPLE HOMES AND TRAILERS ALONG A 0.8 MILE PATH, BEFORE DISSIPATING OVER UNINHABITED AREAS NEAR THE OHOOPEE AND LITTLE OHOOPEE RIVERS.

REPORTS OF TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN SWAINSBORO. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE AROUND TOWN POSSIBLE. TDS NOTED FROM KJGX RADAR.

THE PARENT STORM RESPONSIBLE FOR THE QLCS TORNADO WHICH IMPACTED THE SOUTH SIDE OF COCHRAN QUICKLY REGENERATED A NEW EF1 TORNADO NEAR THE BLECKLEY AND LAURENS COUNTY LINE. HUNDEREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 1.8 MILE TRACK, LIFTING NEAR THOMPSON RD IN LAURENS CO.

THE PARENT STORM WHICH CAUSED THE QLCS TORNADO IN COCHRAN RECYCLED AND GENERATED A NEW EF 1 TORNADO WHICH TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF CHESTER. ROOF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED WITH NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ALONG THE 16.7 MILE TRACK.

20+ TREES REPORTED DOWN ALONG I-16 WITH SMALL CIRCULATION. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

A QLCS EF1 TORNADO FORMED IN SOUTHERN LAURENS COUNTY, MOVING ESE THROUGH A HEAVILY WOODED REGION. 2 LARGE GRAIN BINS WERE DENTED, NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED, A HOME SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE AND A SMALL SHED WAS DESTROYED ALONG THE 4.9 MILE PATH.

THE PARENT STORM WHICH CAUSED THE QLCS TORNADO IN COCHRAN RECYCLED AND GENERATED A NEW EF 1 TORNADO WHICH TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF CHESTER. ROOF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED WITH NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ALONG THE 16.7 MILE TRACK.
SEVERAL ROADS REPORTED CLOSED TO DUE FLASH FLOODING AROUND TALBOT COUNTY PER EM.
TREE DOWN ON POWERLINES ALONG SPRINGDALE RD BETWEEN WAVERLY DR AND BEAVER RUNN.
ROAD FLOODING FROM OVERFLOWING DITCHES NEAR BYRON ALONG HWY 41.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN INCLUDING DAMAGE TO A HOME. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

A FAST-MOVING QLCS PRODUCED A 9.1 MILE LONG TRACKED TORNADO STARTING JUST NORTH OF ADRIAN. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED WITH SOME FALLING ON HOMES. THE TORNADO WEAKENED THEN INTENSIFIED AS IT CROSSED COW FORD BRIDGE RD SNAPPING OR UPROOTING 50-100 TREES OVER A NEARLY 1/2 MILE WIDE SWATH.

THE SAME QLCS THAT PRODUCED THE TORNADO EAST OF ADRIAN ALSO SPAWNED ANOTHER EF-1 TORNADO AT ABOUT THE SAME TIME ABOUT 3.75 MILES WEST OF KITE. HUNDREDS OF TREES, POWERLINES, AND 20-30 HOMES WERE DAMAGED ALONG THE 8.5 MILE TRACK. NOTABLY, A BASEBALL FIELD SUSTAINED HEAVY DAMAGE TO THE CONCESSION BUILDING, FENCING AND STANDS. AT THE CEMETERY ON THE EAST PART OF THE CITY, THREE 500 LB 8 BY 4 FOOT GRANITE MONUMENTS ORIGINALLY LAYING FACE DOWN WERE LIFTED UP AND FLIPPED OVER.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AROUND THE KITE COMMUNITY PER EM. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
POWERLINES DOWN ALONG 400 BLOCK OF MCRAE SCOTLAND RD DUE TO WINDS FROM GUST FRONT.
NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG HWY 80 NEAR KEMP RD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
